steps.title: Use the horizontal shift
steps.content: Adding $540$ to every $x$-value shifts the scatterplot and its line of best fit $540$ units to the right. The slope stays the same.

steps.title: Shift a readable point
steps.content: A readable point on the original best-fit line is about $(100,14)$. After the shift, that point becomes $(640,14)$.

steps.title: Check the shifted model
steps.content:
The model with slope $\frac{35}{1{,}301}$ and intercept $-3$ passes near the shifted point.
$$-3+\frac{35}{1{,}301}(640)\sim 14.2$$
The model with the same slope and intercept $26$ is much too high.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graph the candidate line with intercept $-3$ and slope $\frac{35}{1{,}301}$, then mark the shifted point $(640,14)$. The line stays near the shifted point. The answer is A.
